finding: Plan captain can execute another actor's live task close/write and post-close release publish by setting that actor id, instead of routing the action back to the owning worker lane or requiring an explicit takeover/closeout proxy artifact. ATM currently treats --actor text as sufficient authority in these paths, so a captain can effectively borrow another worker's ticket.
expectedBehavior: ATM should hard-block borrowed actor authority. A task lease, runner-sync ticket, framework claim, close window, commit, and push should be bound to the executing process/lane capability, not just a string --actor value. Captain authority should be able to approve, dispatch, monitor, and arbitrate, but direct mutation on a worker-owned active task must require a governed proxy/takeover receipt that records who approved it, who executed it, why the owner lane did not execute it, and which commands were delegated.
evidenceOrRepro: ATM-GOV-0263 was owned by claude-002-plan31-captain. The captain session executed taskflow close --write as --actor claude-002-plan31-captain, then used framework-mode release artifact publish under that actor, producing target close fd9503eb7396b42dc48a350fe6d2044504e57a61, planning close fda5470d3ebc69ecc97b5c4be86cf05038629348, and release artifact commit 78e0b2a2156d30a9d34dea1f4bffd546471568d1.
followUp: Add a Plan3.1 governance task for captain/worker lane-boundary enforcement: close/write, governed commit, framework-mode, runner-sync, and push should reject actor impersonation or ticket reuse unless a proxy/takeover artifact is present. Add regression coverage that a captain session cannot use another actor's active task lease or runner ticket by passing that actor id. Dispatch skills should also say captain approval is not execution authority.
